work_order.objective:
Delivery strategy: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev
Stage 1/3: creative-dev
Project: aetherion-maze / Aetherion Maze Games
Primary objective: Converge Aetherion Maze under one lead-owned path that runs creative-dev first, then game-dev, then app-dev, with one primary delivery target and no parallel rebuild drift.
Current project summary: Aetherion Maze review lane with Godot web export, legacy prototype references, and Hermes operator tracking.
Current tracking now: n/a

Stage contract:
Freeze the creative target and acceptance language for the primary artifact. Name what to preserve, what to drop, and what the implementation lanes must not drift from.

Prior handoff context:
- No prior specialist output yet. Start from the project brief and live artifact tree.

Required output:
- concise human summary
- machine-readable json block with summary, implemented, verified, assumed, blocked, risks, next_actions, artifacts, handoff_needed

Leave game-dev a clean next action.
Make the first `next_actions` item the concrete handoff for game-dev.

delivery_model.strategy_label: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev

stage_guidance.operator: Own scope, freeze the delivery target, and keep the handoffs honest.
stage_guidance.creative-dev: Define the creative target, vision guardrails, and artifact acceptance language before implementation drifts.
stage_guidance.game-dev: Own the playable build path and converge the actual game implementation on the chosen target artifact.
stage_guidance.app-dev: Own the app, tooling, portal, and review-loop support needed to make the chosen artifact reviewable and repeatable.

handoff_contract: freeze one primary delivery target before downstream implementation expands scope
treat creative-dev as the vision and acceptance guardrail for downstream lanes
treat game-dev as the owner of the playable implementation path once the vision is frozen
treat app-dev as the owner of portal, tooling, packaging, and review-loop support after the build path is clear
require each lane to leave a concrete handoff for the next lane instead of sending the operator back into open-ended review

verification_required: run the smallest local verification possible before claiming success
work_order.project_now: Lead orchestration in motion: Converge Aetherion Maze under one lead-owned path that runs creative-dev first, then game-dev, then app-dev, with one primary delivery target and no parallel rebuild drift.

structured_result.summary: Creative target frozen on the Godot 4 web export. Cozy HUD/theme, vignette, palette, and input cadence are now guardrails. Legacy HTML/Canvas prototypes are reference-only. Acceptance language is codified and a concrete handoff to game-dev is in place.
implemented: Created vision-freeze document specifying the single canonical target (Godot web), tone, pillars to preserve, what to drop, and do-not-drift constraints
Created acceptance guardrails with checkable criteria for HUD/theme, icons, vignette, palette, input pacing, packaging, diagnostics, and performance
Created a game-dev handoff checklist to re-export the Godot web build and verify guardrails
verified: HUD pack assets exist: godot-maze/res/HUD.tres, res/assets/ui/fonts/*, res/assets/ui/icons/*, res/ui/Vignette.tscn, res/assets/ui/shaders/vignette.gdshader
Existing UI docs exist: docs/ui/ui/hud-pack.md, docs/ui/ui/palette-cozy.md
COI dev server script exists at scripts/devserver_coi.py (as referenced by README.md)
assumed: Godot 4.x available in the build environment used by game-dev
NunitoSans variable TTF renders correctly with variation_opentype weights on the target Godot version
Existing web export reflects current scenes (title/main) without hidden debug overlays
risks: Parallel drift: contributors may resume work on legacy HTML/Canvas prototypes; clarified as reference-only but enforcement relies on process
Font variation risk: some Godot builds ignore variation_opentype, causing weight fallback and reduced legibility
Mobile hitbox sizing not runtime-verified yet; D-pad must meet ≥44px targets
Vignette layer ordering may regress during scene changes if not locked below HUD
Performance target (≈60fps) not runtime-measured on modest laptops in this turn
next_actions: {'lane': 'game-dev', 'action': 'Re-export the Godot Web build to /godot-web/ per acceptance-guardrails, verify HUD theme application, icon crispness (18–20px), vignette ordering (below HUD), input pacing (no diagonals; steady cooldown), mobile D-pad ≥44px, and deliver a short QA note of verified guardrails and any deviations.', 'references': ['/home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/creative-dev/aetherion-maze/handoff-game-dev.md', '/home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/creative-dev/aetherion-maze/vision-freeze.md', '/home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/creative-dev/aetherion-maze/acceptance-guardrails.md']}
{'lane': 'creative-dev', 'action': 'Be on deck to adjust HUD outline weight or vignette edge_opacity if game-dev reports legibility/performance issues on their target devices.'}
{'lane': 'game-dev', 'action': 'Run via scripts/devserver_coi.py on :8000 and confirm no console errors in non-debug; attach screenshots of title and in-game HUD at DPR 1 and 3.'}
